description_long: This clock tower features a compact, wooden structure with a dark, weathered finish and a distinct, peaked roof reminiscent of Gothic Revival architecture. The front face displays a large, circular clock with Roman numerals and two black hands, set within a recessed, arched frame. The wood appears aged, with visible grain, scratches, and dark stains, giving it a rustic, historical character. The sides and back are solid, with no visible openings or decorative elements, emphasizing the utilitarian design. The base is broad and sturdy, providing stability, while the overall form is symmetrical and geometric. The clock face is the only interactive or visually prominent feature, with no visible bells, chimes, or entry points.
description: A small, weathered wooden clock tower with a peaked roof and a prominent Roman numeral clock face set in an arched frame. The structure is rustic and symmetrical, with a dark, aged finish.
description_view_0: The back of the clock tower, showing a plain, weathered wooden surface with no clock face or decorative features.
description_view_1: A side view revealing the tower's depth, curved roof profile, and the solid, textured wooden surface.
description_view_2: The front view, featuring the large, circular clock face with Roman numerals and the arched wooden frame.
description_view_3: The opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the curved roof and solid wood construction.
attribute_clock_face_design: The clock face is circular, set within an arched wooden frame, and features black Roman numerals on a beige background. It has two black hands (hour and minute) and no additional markings or decorative elements. The design is simple and traditional.
